text: Top tier of HFACS: organizational climate, operational process, resource management. Failures here propagate down through supervision to unsafe acts. Examples: production pressure that overrides safety, inadequate training budget, accepted normalization of deviance.

Inadequate supervision, planned inappropriate operations, failed to correct known problem, supervisory violations. Examples: dispatching a flight knowing weather is below mins, failing to address repeated training failures.

Environmental factors (physical environment, technological), condition of operators (fatigue, mental state), personnel factors (crew resource management, personal readiness). The 'set up' for the unsafe act.
Errors (skill-based / decision / perceptual) and violations (routine / exceptional). The immediate cause. Always present in an incident, but rarely the root cause.
Controlled Flight Into Terrain. Airworthy aircraft inadvertently flown into terrain. Common preconditions: night IMC, lack of TAWS, missed approach mishandled.

Loss of Control - Inflight. Aircraft exceeds operational design envelope (stall, spin, departure from controlled flight). Common preconditions: distraction, hand-flying complex situations, atmospheric upset.

International standard for accident / incident investigation. Sole objective is prevention, NOT blame. Requires investigation by independent authority of the State of Occurrence. Final report follows standardized structure.

SMS = Safety Management System. 4 components: (1) Safety Policy, (2) Safety Risk Management (hazard ID + risk assessment + mitigation), (3) Safety Assurance (continuous monitoring + audit), (4) Safety Promotion (training + communication). Mandatory for Part 121 + 135 operators in many countries.

Advisory Circular for SMS for Part 121 air carriers. Implements the ICAO SMS framework with US-specific guidance. Required for Part 121 since 2018. Includes voluntary safety-reporting programs (ASAP).

Aviation Safety Action Program. Voluntary employee safety-reporting program with FAA + operator + employee representatives. Reports go to Event Review Committee (ERC). Protections: no enforcement action for sole-source-of-knowledge reports (with exceptions for: criminal activity, drug/alcohol, intentional disregard, falsified info).

Categories A (serious incident, collision avoided by extreme action), B (significant separation lost), C (separation issue), D (no immediate safety consequences). All categories investigated; A and B require formal NOTAM + cause analysis.

Flight Time, Duty Period, and Rest Requirements (FAR 117). Maximum flight duty period varies by start time + segments + augmentation. Minimum 10-hour rest before flight duty (8 hours uninterrupted sleep opportunity). Cumulative limits: 100 hrs in 28 days, 1000 hrs in 365 days.
